chunks.text: The UC Berkeley Women in Computer Science and Engineering (WiCSE) 40th Anniversary Reunion, which celebrates 40 years of women in technology at Berkeley, will be held on Saturday, March 17, 2018. Join us for a day packed full of reunions, enlightening talks, and networking opportunities!
chunks.question: When was the WiCSE 40th Anniversary Reunion held?

chunks.text: Big Give, UC Berkeley’s annual fundraising outreach to alumni, students, parents, faculty, staff, and friends will happen between 9 pm on Wednesday, March 13 and 9 pm on Thursday, March 14! With your help, we can remain one of the finest departments in the No. 1 public university in the world. We hope you will join us!
chunks.question: What is the purpose of Big Give?
chunks.answer: Annual fundraising outreach for UC Berkeley

chunks.text: After nearly 50 years of Moore’s Law, and many premature predictions of its demise along the way, it is now clear that “conventional” transistor scaling is finally reaching fundamental limits. This year’s BEARS conference will highlight some of the latest thinking and results that show a bright future for electronics and computing.
chunks.question: What does the BEARS 2018 conference focus on?
chunks.answer: Future of electronics and computing beyond Moore's Law

chunks.text: The Symposium of Fuzzy Logic and Fuzzy Sets on February 5, 2018, honors of the life and research of pioneer Lotfi Zadeh, widely recognized as the “Father of Fuzzy Logic.” Speakers will discuss Zadeh’s wide-ranging influence and lasting contributions to computer science, statistical analysis, and the foundations of mathematics.
chunks.question: Who is honored at the February 5, 2018 symposium?
chunks.answer: Lotfi Zadeh, Father of Fuzzy Logic
